content: The intrinsic reward modules in curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (ICM, RND) can be repurposed as a dynamic memory pruning gate for SOTA 2024-2025 active learning agents, where prediction error (the core signal of curiosity) identifies which unlabeled memory trajectories are both high-information for active learning and worth retaining in limited agent memory. This replaces static memory pruning rules that currently waste capacity on low-value unlabeled experiences.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
